title: Shared operator status, data-source, readiness, evidence, and authority taxonomy
scope: @ontogony/ui shared status vocabulary and rendering primitives
ontogony-frontend adoption across Home, System, Kanon, Conexus, Allagma, Evidence Spine, Agent Interaction, topology, settings, and evaluation surfaces
copy rules that prevent generic unknown, misleading healthy/ready language, fixture-as-live claims, and debug/API language in normal operator views
nonGoals: Do not change service behavior except where minimal DTO fields are already available and only need adapter mapping.
Do not hide real failures behind softer wording.
Do not introduce production-security claims beyond the actual local/alpha posture.
Do not make fixture/generated/demo evidence count as live readiness.
acceptanceSummary: No operator page displays bare 'unknown' without a label and reason.
Connectivity, readiness, contract health, operator usability, evidence completeness, data source, and authority are represented as separate dimensions.
Shared taxonomy is implemented in @ontogony/ui and consumed by ontogony-frontend pages.
Fixture/demo/generated/imported/live states are consistently badged and never used as page headlines.
Topology separates current, validated, degraded, missing, and planned edges.
Regression checks catch forbidden misleading copy.
